title: 3D Reprojection-Driven Robot Navigation Improves Depth Sensing
container-title: 2025 International Conference on Advanced Robotics and Mechatronics (ICARM)

title: Enhancing Human-Robot Teleoperation through Depth Sensing and eXtended Reality
container-title: 2025 IEEE Conference on Virtual Reality and 3D User Interfaces Abstracts and Workshops (VRW)

abstract:
<jats:p>Abstract
GPS-denied navigation forces robots to close the perception–action loop using onboard sensing, imperfect maps, and uncertain dynamics, often under strict power and latency limits. As sensing approaches fundamental limits—photon shot noise in vision, thermal noise in inertial sensors, and quantum noise in emerging magnetometers—the bottleneck shifts from “more data” to “better decisions with quantified uncertainty.” This manuscript develops a publisher-ready framework for quantum-limited sensing-to-action: how to design measurement models, fuse information, and select actions so that navigation performance claims are evidence-bearing and deployable in safety-relevant settings. The central argument is that quantum-limited operation is primarily an evaluation and governance challenge: algorithms must be linked to assumptions about noise, calibration drift, and environmental shift, with explicit validity logic and lifecycle controls. The text connects (i) foundations, uncertainty, and research design for GPS-denied navigation; (ii) statistical modeling for explanation and causal inference in localization and mapping outcomes; (iii) machine learning for prediction, generalization, and trustworthiness in perception and planning; (iv) scalable data engineering and MLOps for fielded autonomy; and (v) sectoral horizons, policy impact, and governance artifacts that translate technical evidence into accountable adoption. The manuscript avoids unverifiable case claims and instead specifies protocols, metrics, and decision tools suitable for researchers, practitioners, and policymakers.
Keywords
GPS-denied navigation, quantum-limited sensing, inertial navigation, visual-inertial odometry, simultaneous localization and mapping, sensor fusion, uncertainty quantification, probabilistic robotics, causal inference, trustworthy machine learning, domain shift, calibration, robustness, safety constraints, real-time planning, scalable analytics, reproducibility, MLOps, governance, assurance cases</jats:p>
title: Quantum-Limited Sensing-to-Action for GPS-Denied Robot Navigation

publisher: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE)
